channel.title: The Retreat Register — Updates
channel.description: Verification progress from The Retreat Register, published with real numbers: coverage snapshots, data findings, and what gets cut from the register and why.

item.title: Digital-detox retreats: what phone-free actually means at a venue
item.description: The anti-tech mood is not a phase. What separates a real phone-free retreat from a resort with a polite suggestion — policy by policy.

item.title: Dark-sky retreats: darkness is the amenity now
item.description: Certified dark-sky zones, cabins built around the night sky, and why the best amenity at a growing number of venues is what you can't see.

item.title: The nervous-system reset retreat: the product is subtraction
item.description: No workshops, no icebreakers, no agenda. One of the year's rising retreat formats does less on purpose — here's how to spot a real one.

item.title: Couples retreat venues: the real kind, not the movie
item.description: The fastest-growing retreat segment in BookRetreats' industry survey is two people and a cabin. What actually separates a couples-ready venue from a resort with a nice brochure.

item.title: Retreat planning when the travel warnings are loud
item.description: Advisories are spiking searches this week. A quiet fact: a retreat you can drive to skips the country-advisory question entirely. The case for staying domestic.

item.title: Corporate retreat venues: how to shortlist without losing a week
item.description: Company retreat planning starts with a venue shortlist. Here is the four-ask pass that replaces a week of tabs and unanswered forms.

item.title: Why venue capacity numbers are missing everywhere — including here
item.description: Capacity looks like a simple number. It isn't — and pretending otherwise is how planners end up with 60 people and seating for 45.

item.title: Faith-based retreat centers: in the register, held to the same bar
item.description: Congregation getaways are a core retreat use — and faith-based centers earn their listings exactly like everyone else.

item.title: The small-market advantage nobody prices in
item.description: Famous destinations fight over your dates. Small markets don't. Why the register sweeps towns the big platforms skip.

item.title: The full buyout: the most misunderstood option in retreat planning
item.description: Taking the whole property changes everything — price, privacy, and what your team can actually do. Here's how buyouts really work.

item.title: The fastest venue quality test: can you actually reach it?
item.description: A surprising share of venue listings across the web dead-end at contact forms and chatbots. Reachability is a quality signal — and the register measures it.

item.title: Venue photos lie more than any other part of a listing
item.description: Borrowed photos, decade-old shots, and another property's sunset — why the register machine-checks every photo set against the venue it claims to show.

item.title: Five questions to ask a retreat venue before you sign anything
item.description: The five answers that decide your real cost and your real experience — and why most planners ask none of them until it's too late.

item.title: The Build Log: day by day since day one
item.description: One dated entry per day since the pipeline first switched on — venues verified, junk cut, coverage targets, quality passes. Updated daily until launch.

item.title: We re-judged our own rejections. The real venues earned their way back.
item.description: A directory that only adds is a list; one that corrects itself is a reference work. Inside the register's biggest quality pass — both directions.

item.title: Most retreat venues won't show you a price — here's how to read that
item.description: Most verified venues do not publish a starting price. That's not hiding — it's how group pricing works, and planners can use it.

item.title: Where the register stands in July
item.description: The first public coverage snapshot: which states run deepest, what got cut this month, and what the machine is scouting right now.
